task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'docker exec -it wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 bash' or a shell prompt indicating the user is inside the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 container.

task_completion.description: Open the local_rules.xml file for editing
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a text editor (e.g., nano, vim) or a command (e.g., cat, grep) accessing '/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml'.

task_completion.description: Fix the XML syntax, invalid rule level, and duplicate rule ID
task_completion.visual_evidence: An editor or terminal output showing the corrected XML content. Look for valid XML tags, valid rule levels (e.g., level="3"), and distinct rule IDs specifically showing '<rule id="100200"' and '<rule id="100201"'.

task_completion.description: Restart the Wazuh Manager service to apply changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a restart command such as '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart' or 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager', followed by output indicating the service started successfully without errors.

task_completion.description: Verify that custom rules 100200 and 100201 are loaded and active
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output from a tool like '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest', grep on Wazuh logs, or the Wazuh dashboard showing that rules 100200 and 100201 are successfully loaded and active.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a real terminal to interact with the container
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard terminal prompts, realistic command execution times, and genuine standard output/error messages rather than manually typed mock outputs.

integrity.description: Verify the rules file was modified inside the actual container
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml' is visible in the editor or command line while inside the 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' container environment, not a local host file.

integrity.description: Verify the Wazuh manager restart sequence is authentic
integrity.visual_evidence: The restart command produces the standard multi-line Wazuh control output (e.g., stopping/starting wazuh-modulesd, wazuh-analysisd, etc.) rather than just a generic success echo.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target container: wazuh-wazuh.manager-1. Target file: /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml. The broken configuration contains an XML syntax error, an invalid rule level, and a duplicate rule ID (100200). The agent must fix these errors, changing the duplicate ID to 100201, and restart the service so that rules 100200 and 100201 are active.
